step.actionDisplay: UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> RESET

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 7 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP.
You are still on the same level.
The game is over.
Current state: step 373, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Game over after UP. Black F and charcoal F swapped panels again. Need to understand game over condition and restart.
- Goal model: Need to restart and find correct sequence to overlap black F with yellow F.
- Plan: Check current state, then try to recover or restart.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 372 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 11.6x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 87 actions returned the board to a state already seen 87 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 87 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state:
- Black F: [15,18]-[23,26] (left panel)
- Charcoal F: [15,36]-[23,44] (right panel)
- Yellow F: [45,51]-[53,59] (right panel, bottom)
- Yellow bar at bottom: [0,63]-[63,63] (timer bar, now yellow instead of black)
The game is over. The black F is on the left panel and the charcoal F is on the right panel. This is the opposite of the initial state.
The timer bar at the bottom is now yellow, which might indicate game over.
I need to understand what caused game over. Looking at the history:
- Before the UP sequence, the black F was at [45,51]-[53,59] and the charcoal F was at [45,3]-[53,11].
- After 7 UP actions, the black F moved to [15,18]-[23,26] and the charcoal F moved to [15,36]-[23,44].
- The F shapes swapped panels!
So the game over condition is when the black F moves UP too far, causing it to swap panels with the charcoal F.
Actually, looking more carefully, the black F and charcoal F swapped positions. The black F was on the right panel at [45,51]-[53,59] and the charcoal F was on the left panel at [45,3]-[53,11]. After moving UP 7 times, the black F is now on the left panel at [15,18]-[23,26] and the charcoal F is on the right panel at [15,36]-[23,44].
This is confusing. Let me think about what happened:
1. Initially, black F was on left panel, charcoal F on right panel.
2. I moved RIGHT, which caused black F to cross the divider and swap with charcoal F.
3. Then I moved the black F (now on right panel) to overlap with yellow F.
4. Then I moved UP, which caused the black F to move up and somehow swap back with the charcoal F.
Wait, maybe the game over condition is when the black F moves UP past a certain row. Let me check the row range.
The black F was at rows 45-53 and moved to rows 15-23 after 7 UP actions. Each UP moves 3 rows, so 7 UP moves 21 rows. 45 - 21 = 24, but the black F is at rows 15-23. So the black F moved up 30 rows (from 45 to 15), which is 10 UP actions worth. But only 7 UP actions were executed.
Actually, looking at the result, 7 UP actions were executed and then game over stopped the sequence. The black F moved from rows 45-53 to rows 15-23, which is a 30-row change. But 7 UP actions should only move 21 rows (7 * 3 = 21). So there's something else happening.
Wait, maybe the black F and charcoal F swapped panels during the UP movement, and the game over condition is when they swap.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics:
1. The black F and charcoal F start on opposite panels.
2. When the black F crosses the divider (RIGHT or LEFT), it swaps with the charcoal F.
3. When the black F moves UP too far, it causes game over.
Actually, looking at the current state, the black F is at [15,18]-[23,26] on the left panel, and the charcoal F is at [15,36]-[23,44] on the right panel. This is similar to the initial state, but the black F and charcoal F have swapped panels.
The initial state had:
- Black F on left panel
- Charcoal F on right panel
- Yellow F on right panel (bottom)
The current state has:
- Black F on left panel
- Charcoal F on right panel
- Yellow F on right panel (bottom)
Wait, that's the same configuration! The black F is on the left panel and the charcoal F is on the right panel. But the positions are different:
- Initial: black F at [15,18]-[23,26], charcoal F at [15,36]-[23,44]
- Current: black F at [15,18]-[23,26], charcoal F at [15,36]-[23,44]
These are the same positions! So the game over state is the initial state. This means the game reset to the initial state after game over.
So the game over condition caused a reset to the initial state. Now I need to find the correct sequence to complete the level.
Let me reconsider the goal. The goal is to get the black F to overlap with the yellow F. The yellow F is at [45,51]-[53,59] on the right panel.
To do this:
1. Move the black F DOWN to rows 45-53 (from rows 15-23, need to move down 30 rows, which is 10 DOWN actions).
2. Move the black F RIGHT to cross the divider and swap with the charcoal F.
3. Move the black F to overlap with the yellow F.
But wait, when the black F crosses the divider (RIGHT), it swaps with the charcoal F. So after crossing, the black F would be on the right panel and the charcoal F would be on the left panel.
Then I need to move the black F to overlap with the yellow F.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is to have the black F at the yellow F's position, and the charcoal F at a corresponding position on the left panel.
Currently, the black F is at [15,18]-[23,26] and the charcoal F is at [15,36]-[23,44].
The yellow F is at [45,51]-[53,59].
To get the black F to [45,51]-[53,59]:
1. Move DOWN 10 to get to rows 45-53 (from rows 15-23).
2. Move RIGHT to cross the divider.
But when the black F crosses the divider, it swaps with the charcoal F. So after crossing, the black F would be on the right panel at a position corresponding to where the charcoal F was.
Let me think about this more carefully. The initial state has:
- Black F at [15,18]-[23,26] (left panel)
- Charcoal F at [15,36]-[23,44] (right panel)
When I move RIGHT, the black F crosses the divider and swaps with the charcoal F. So:
- Black F moves to right panel at [15,36]-[23,44] (where charcoal F was)
- Charcoal F moves to left panel at [15,18]-[23,26] (where black F was)
But earlier, when I moved RIGHT from the initial state, the black F ended up at [45,48]-[53,56] and the charcoal F ended up at [45,6]-[53,14]. This doesn't match the swap pattern.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the movement pattern is different. Let me track the positions more carefully.
Actually, I think I need to restart and carefully track the positions. Let me try a simple sequence: DOWN 10, RIGHT 1.

sections.content:

Goal model: Get black F to overlap with yellow F at [45,51]-[53,59].
Plan: DOWN 10 to move black F to rows 45-53, then RIGHT to cross divider.
